- Enable ruff C901 rule (max-complexity=10) in pyproject.toml to
  enforce cyclomatic complexity as stated in constitution

  refactor (handle_authorization, post_with_retry)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

Docs: amend constitution to v2.0.0 (principle restructure)

Restructure principles to match turnovercal constitution format:
- Expand Code Quality with docstring, type annotation, complexity reqs
- Split TDD into dedicated Test-Driven Development and Phased
  Development principles
- Merge Pre-Commit Integrity into Atomic Commits & Compliance
- Resolve all TODOs: fill UX rules with captive-portal specifics,
  add concrete performance baselines from spec
- Add captive-portal constraints (Argon2, HA add-on, audit logging)

Feature/collision resolution (#11)

- Implement integration tests for the organize command, covering various scenarios including file organization by type, dry-run mode, manifest generation, naming conventions, and error handling.
- Implement integration tests for the scan command, verifying functionality for scanning directories, nested structures, file type filtering, and error handling.

fix(scan): enhance config loading mechanism

fix(manifest): add backward compatibility for manifest structure

- Update the manifest generator to include an operations property as an alias for entries for backward compatibility.

fix(operation): prevent execution of operations when no operations are present

- Add a critical early return in the operation executor to avoid executing performOperation when there are no operations to perform.

commit.message:
SCRUM-79: Fix Commit Messages (#26)

* revamped rust module prototype

* Checkpoint 2 for commit msg refactor. Consider pipeline.md for restructure

* Phase 2 complete of plan.md

* refactor(desktop): migrate all ALS interpretation to Rust NAPI parser

- Add diff_from_snapshot() export: diffs committed snapshot JSON against
  current ALS file, skipping HEAD blob download on the fast path
- Add generateCommitMessage() export: ports buildCommitMessage() logic
  from JS to Rust, removing the last JS function that read DiffReport data
- Convert all NAPI exports to async via tokio::task::spawn_blocking,
  keeping the Electron main process unblocked during ALS parsing
- Add schema_version field to Project struct with serde default for
  backwards-compatible snapshot deserialization
- Rewrite project.ts from ~800 lines to 61: remove all fast-xml-parser-
  based XML parsing, decompression, structuralCompareAls, buildLocalDiffFromAls,
  getAlsContent, and related helpers; retain only gitBin + git shell commands
- Replace get-als-content and no-commits fallback handlers with parseAls
- Remove buildCommitMessage() from main.ts
- Delete 9 orphaned types from types/index.ts (DecompressedAls, AlsChange,
  StructuralCompareResult, AudioTrackEntry, MidiTrackEntry, etc.)
- Uninstall fast-xml-parser (removes 68 packages)

JS is now purely transport: paths and buffers only. All ALS semantics
live in the Rust crate. One parser to update if Ableton's format changes.

* chore(desktop): remove old dist/vendor parser, restore git binary

* Fix build and ordering issue by implementing sorting the hashmap

* Remove outdated PIPELINE Claude file.

* Update apps/desktop/native/semantic-diff/src/diff.rs

Fix issue of unexpected characters when overflowing integers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update "children" model for the als structure to use a default so it doesn't fail in the event of empty children

production code.

be used in linted source files. Build scripts and test configurations
remain unaffected as they fall outside the linting scope.

"rules": {

feat: scoring overhaul — criteria-based pass/fail, report improvements

Major changes across 10 issues:

Issues 8, 10: Scoring overhaul
- Replace 1-10 dimension scores with pass/fail per criterion
- ReviewScores now holds []CriterionResult (name, passed, reason)
- Score = count of passed criteria / total criteria
- Pass = all criteria met, Fail = any criterion not met

commit.message:
feat: multi-model review panel with consolidated scoring

Implements a "jury" review system where multiple LLM models independently
review generated code, then a consolidator synthesizes consensus scores.

Architecture:
- Config: reviewer_models: [list] replaces reviewer_model (backward compat kept)
- First model in list acts as the consolidator
- All reviewers run in parallel (goroutines with separate Copilot clients)
- Consolidator receives all individual reviews and produces consensus
- Fallback: if consolidation fails, uses median scores

Changes:
- config.go: Add ReviewerModels field + EffectiveReviewerModels() method
- config.go: Updated validation (no model overlap, no duplicates)
- reviewer.go: Add PanelReviewer with ReviewPanel() and consolidation
- types.go: Add Model field to ReviewResult, ReviewPanel to EvalReport
- engine.go: Wire panel reviewer, fix duration to track generation only
- html.go: Add review panel table showing per-reviewer + consensus scores
- main.go: Wire panel creation when reviewer_models has 2+ entries
- All configs updated with reviewer_models: [claude-opus-4.6, gpt-4.1]

commit.message:
feat: Add torsion detection, OpenMM forces, and improper support

Implement three tightly coupled features that close issues #127,
#112, and #113:

Phase 1 — Torsion detection (#127):
- DetectedTorsion dataclass in molecule.py with canonical element_quad
- _detect_torsions() walks bond graph to find A-B-C-D quadruples
- Lazy torsions property on Q2MMMolecule (parallel to bonds/angles)
- canonicalize_torsion_env_id() in identifiers.py
- ForceField.match_torsion() with ff_row/env_id/elements fallback

Phase 2 — OpenMM torsion forces (#112):
- _TorsionTerm dataclass and torsion_force/torsion_terms on OpenMMHandle
- PeriodicTorsionForce creation in create_context()
- Torsion parameter updates in update_forcefield()

Phase 3 — Improper torsion support (#113):
- is_improper boolean on TorsionParam (replaces label-string checking)
- AMBER frcmod loader sets is_improper=True for IMPROPER section
- proper_torsions/improper_torsions properties on ForceField
- match_torsion() accepts is_improper filter
- OpenMM assigns improper terms from FF (not geometry detection)

Closes #127, closes #112, closes #113

commit.message:
[Tooltips] Fix positioning to never overflow viewport (#13230)

### This PR improves tooltip behavior: better positioning, smarter
viewport handling, and some minor cleanup.

**Viewport-aware positioning**
> Added a `clampToViewport` function that keeps tooltips from spilling
outside the screen. It flips the tooltip to the opposite side if needed,
and nudges it back within the viewport margins before applying the
position.

**Removal and event handling**
> `removeTooltips` now has a guard clause so it won't throw if the
tooltip is already gone. The `mouseenter` handler is simplified, and
tooltips are properly cleaned up on navigation and frame loads.

**Cleanup**
> Removed some stale comments and tightened up the class sync logic when
showing tooltips.

commit.message:
Address PR #1856 review feedback: fix fail-open, tree-ish parsing, pathspec-from-file

Fix three issues identified in code review:

1. HIGH: AddStagedFilesToModifiedPaths now returns failure when
   git diff --cached exits non-zero, instead of silently succeeding
   with addedCount=0 (which re-opened the corruption this PR fixes).

2. MEDIUM: GetRestorePathspec now strips the tree-ish argument for
   checkout commands (e.g. HEAD in 'checkout HEAD -- file'), and
   skips --source/-s values for restore commands, preventing
   accidental pathspec scope widening.

3. MEDIUM: --pathspec-from-file and --pathspec-file-nul are now
   forwarded through the IPC protocol to the mount process, which
   passes them directly to git diff --cached. The hook never reads
   the file itself -- git handles all pathspec parsing on both sides.
   Stdin (-) is unsupported in hook context and fails closed.

Refactors parsing logic into UnstageCommandParser (public static class
in a separate file) so it can be linked into GVFS.UnitTests without
pulling in the full GVFS.Hooks Exe assembly.

DiffCachedNameStatus now accepts optional pathspecFromFile and
pathspecFileNul parameters.

IPC message body format extended:
  null/empty        -> all staged files
  path1\0path2      -> inline pathspecs (unchanged)
  \nF\n<filepath>   -> --pathspec-from-file
  \nFZ\n<filepath>  -> --pathspec-from-file + --pathspec-file-nul

Adds 28 unit tests covering IsUnstageOperation and GetRestorePathspec.

commit.message:
Reuse test/db JSON files as single source of seed data

Replace the hardcoded SQL seed file with a shell script that reads the
JSON files from test/db/ using PostgreSQL's json_to_recordset(). This
ensures the init scripts and test fixtures always stay in sync.

- Mount test/db/ into the container at /testdata
- docker/initdb/02-seed.sh reads JSON and inserts via psql
- Remove docker/initdb/02-seed.sql (no more duplicated data)
